diagrams.title: 1D Array Memory Layout
diagrams.description: Shows how a 1D array with 5 elements is stored in contiguous memory locations
diagrams.purpose: Understand contiguous memory allocation and indexing in arrays

diagrams.title: 2D Array in Row-Major Order
diagrams.description: Visualizes a 2D array arr[3][4] stored in row-major order with element positions
elements: Row 0: arr[0][0], arr[0][1], arr[0][2], arr[0][3] stored consecutively
Row 1: arr[1][0], arr[1][1], arr[1][2], arr[1][3] after row 0
Row 2: arr[2][0], arr[2][1], arr[2][2], arr[2][3] after row 1
Memory addresses increase row by row
diagrams.purpose: Understand row-major ordering and memory layout of 2D arrays

diagrams.title: Array Operations Time Complexity
diagrams.description: Cheat sheet showing time complexity of different operations on arrays
elements: Insert at end: O(1) amortized for dynamic, O(1) for static if space
Insert at beginning: O(n) - must shift all elements
Delete at beginning: O(n) - must shift elements left
diagrams.purpose: Quick reference for array operation complexities

diagrams.title: Dynamic Array Resizing
diagrams.description: Shows the process of dynamic array resizing when capacity is reached
elements: Initial capacity: 4, elements added one by one
When full (4 elements), create new array of capacity 8
Copy all 4 elements to new array - O(n) operation
Now can add more elements with O(1) amortized cost
diagrams.purpose: Understand how dynamic arrays handle growth and the concept of amortization
